title: Grand Rapids · A Quarter-Century of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 2000–2022 + GRPD NIBRS 2023–2026 · aggregated by police beat

hook.sub: FBI UCR · Violent + Property · fully sourced

copy.chapter2Title: The map comes alive — per police beat
copy.chapter2Caption: Live monthly reports by neighborhood. Dots show density within a beat, not exact spots.
copy.transitionDesc: From here the data is incident-level and per police beat — four NIBRS categories, real monthly counts, distributed as density within each beat.
copy.methodDotsSub: 96.7% mapped to 33 beats — the rest counted, disclosed, never invented
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2022) and GRPD NIBRS (2023+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which neighborhood is Grand Rapids' safest?
copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2022 the gray bars are the FBI's yearly tally of Grand Rapids' classic “index” crimes — murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft. From 2023 the figures switch to GRPD's NIBRS “Group A” offenses — an incident-based system covering a broader list of crimes — so the level steps up because the measure widened, not because crime jumped. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: GRPD · City of Grand Rapids ArcGIS Hub · aggregated per beat · no incidents plotted
copy.creditsSources: Sources: Grand Rapids Police Department crime data (2023–) & FBI UCR (2000–2022) via the City of Grand Rapids ArcGIS Hub. Beat polygons: GRPD Service Area Map. Neighborhood names: City of Grand Rapids Neighborhood Areas. Used under the City of Grand Rapids GIS Data Access & Use Constraint Agreement (provided "as is"). Basemap (highways & landmarks): © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1991: violent crime peaks at 3,278 reports — the height of the early-'90s wave.
Property crime fell sharply through the 2010s — from 10,942 in 2000 toward 3,869 by 2018.
2018: property crime bottoms out at 3,869 — about 65% below its 2000 level.
2020: violent crime jumps about 50% (1,299 → 1,951); property rises too.

annotations.text: Each dot shows how many — spread within the beat to show density, not where a crime happened.
July 2023 is the highest month for Group A crime — 1,680 incidents citywide.
Property crime peaks in summer: ~663 reports/month in Jun–Aug versus ~503 in winter.
Beat CENTRAL 3 (Oldtown-Heartside, downtown) is the busiest single police beat for Group A crime. Larger multi-beat neighborhoods total more — see the finish.
May 2026 is the highest month on record for Crimes Against Persons — 628 reports.
